exampleSentences: Sáng nay anh bị mắc kẹt trong thang máy gần nửa tiếng.
Chiếc xe của em bị mắc kẹt trên đường ngập nước sau cơn mưa lớn.
Chị cảm thấy mắc kẹt với quá nhiều công việc cần hoàn thành hôm nay.

translation.en: I am stuck in the elevator.

notes:
Mắc kẹt means stuck — physically unable to move or get out. It can also mean feeling trapped in a situation.

It’s often used with bị when something bad happens unexpectedly. You can use it for vehicles, people, objects, or abstract situations (work, relationships, problems).

Close synonym: kẹt (shorter, more casual). Opposite idea: thoát ra / thoát khỏi (get out, escape).
notes_md:
**Mắc kẹt** means *stuck* — physically unable to move or get out. It can also mean feeling trapped in a situation.

It’s often used with **bị** when something bad happens unexpectedly. You can use it for vehicles, people, objects, or abstract situations (work, relationships, problems).


Close synonym: **kẹt** (shorter, more casual). Opposite idea: **thoát ra / thoát khỏi** (get out, escape).

translation.en: I am stuck in the elevator.

definitions.es: No poder moverse o escapar de una situación.
definitions.en: To be unable to move or escape from a situation.
definitions.vi: Không thể di chuyển hoặc thoát khỏi một tình huống.

example.vi: Tôi bị mắc kẹt trong giao thông.

exampleSentencesTranslated: This morning I was stuck in the elevator for almost half an hour.
My car was stuck on the flooded street after the heavy rain.
She feels stuck with too much work to finish today.
